# Proposed Issue Closures — June 2026

*Generated by comparing ROADMAP (#52), Intake Pipeline Phase 2 plan (#88), and all open issues. Cross-referenced against current codebase implementation.*

---

## Summary

After completing WU 1.9 (issues #79–#90), several older open issues became fully or substantially resolved as a side-effect of the work. These are not explicitly tracked in the roadmap tables and remain open in GitHub. This document identifies them for closure.

---

## Tier 1 — Close Immediately (Fully Resolved)

These issues describe bugs or features that are provably implemented in the current codebase. Each entry includes the exact code location that satisfies the requirement.

---

### #69 — Bug: ROUND corrections create cascade notes instead of patching round data

**Original problem:** `CORRECTION_SYSTEM` had no schema for ROUND items. The AI couldn't produce valid slot-level diffs, so it fell back to creating cascade notes. `applyDiff()` had no bracket-path handler for nested ROUND writes.

**What resolved it:** Issue #86 (`Delta Review: Send full slot structure as JSON context`)

**Evidence in code:**
- `shared/chronicle-ai.js`: `CORRECTION_SYSTEM` extended with full bracket-path diff examples AND the new `reorder` op spec — AI now has explicit schema for slot-level writes
- `admin/delta-review.html` `applyDiff()`: bracket-path parser added for `slots[n].field` paths; reorder handler added for `{op:"reorder", slots:[...]}` diffs
- `sendCorrectionToAI()`: ROUND items now receive the full untruncated `rawData` JSON + PARTY ROSTER block

**Recommendation:** Close with comment linking to #86. Note that structural reordering (the hard case) is now also supported via the reorder op — the original bug is comprehensively resolved.

---

### #70 — Bug: OCR pages silently skipped on publish — combat rounds never update campaign JSON

**Original problem:** `applyDeltasToCampaign()` had no handler for `type: 'RAW'` items. Every approved OCR page produced zero change to the campaign JSON. Combat rounds were never written.

**What resolved it:** Two changes:
1. Issue #73: Added the Interpret button to RAW item cards (Stage B of the proposed fix)
2. The Interpret flow in `interpretRawItem()` converts OCR text → structured ROUND delta items that ARE written on publish

**Evidence in code:**
- `admin/delta-review.html`: RAW items display an amber warning banner ("OCR pages are not written to the campaign on publish") — Stage A satisfied
- `admin/delta-review.html` `interpretRawItem()`: sends OCR text to `fillRoundsFromText()`, wraps each proposal as a `type: 'ROUND'` cascade item that routes correctly through `applyDeltasToCampaign()` — Stage B satisfied
- README: explicitly documents the RAW → Interpret → ROUND flow under Delta Review

**Recommendation:** Close with comment explaining the implemented solution: RAW items are intentionally not written directly; the Interpret button produces ROUND items that are. The original "silent skip" is now a visible workflow with an explicit conversion step.

---

### #64 — Bug: Session title and summary published empty with no warning

**Original problem:** The session stub sent from intake had `title: ''` and `summary: ''` as placeholders. No fill step existed. Approving the stub without editing committed empty strings to campaign JSON.

**What resolved it:** Issue #85 (`Delta Review: Inline edit for entity cards and session stub fields`)

**Evidence in code:**
- `admin/delta-review.html`: session stub card has inline-editable `<span>` elements for title and summary (`updateSessionStubField()` writes to `rawData` on commit)
- `setItemStatus()`: checks `item._titleEmpty` and blocks approval with an inline warning when title is blank — the fix from the issue's Option A is implemented
- The `_titleEmpty` flag is set and cleared by `renderReview()` / `updateSessionStubField()` so the warning appears on the same render pass that blocks approval

**Recommendation:** Close with comment linking to #85.

---

### #72 — Bug: RAW item main card does not update after AI correction is applied

**Original problem:** The RAW item `render` closure in `loadIntakeDelta()` was an arrow function capturing `ocrText` as a local constant. When `applyDiff()` mutated `rawData.ocr_text`, `renderReview()` still showed the original captured value.

**What resolved it:** Fixed directly during WU 1.9 work (specifically during the #85/#83 cycle).

**Evidence in code:**
- `admin/delta-review.html` `loadIntakeDelta()` RAW branch: `render` is now a regular function (`render = function() { const ocrText = this.rawData?.ocr_text || '(empty)'; ... }`)
- Code comment explicitly attributes the fix: *"Regular function, not arrow — applyDiff() mutates rawData.ocr_text in-place... `this` is the item object because renderReview() calls item.nlRender() as a method (issue #72)."*

**Recommendation:** Close with comment noting the fix is in `loadIntakeDelta()` RAW branch and referencing the inline code comment.

---

### #8 — Delta Review: Add pre-publish integrity panel

**Original problem:** No integrity check before publish. Gaps in combat rounds could be committed silently.

**What resolved it:** The integrity panel was fully implemented. It is described in the README and is the currently-running feature in delta-review.html.

**Evidence in code/README:**
- `admin/delta-review.html` `initIntegrity()`: fetches campaign JSON, runs `ChronicleIntegrity.checks()`, renders the gap panel
- Accept/Defer/Edit triad: all three buttons implemented; Defer creates `deferred_gaps[]` entries
- Publish blocking: `canPublish()` gated on all gaps having explicit Accept or Defer decisions
- Fill proposals: `approveGapProposal()` wraps AI proposals as ROUND delta items in the approval queue
- Reprocess placeholder: disabled button with full design-question comment block

**Two acceptance criteria remain unmet** — tracked as separate open bugs:
- #38: partial resolution state (gap partially filled — distinct visual state not yet implemented)
- #39: integrity fill rounds corrupt on publish (wrong `type` in `applyDeltasToCampaign()`)

**Recommendation:** Close #8 as implemented. The remaining deficiencies are tracked in #38 and #39, which are active bugs in WU 1.1. Closing #8 correctly reflects that the feature was built; the bugs are a separate concern.

---

## Tier 2 — Close with Caveat (Substantially Addressed, One Gap Remains)

---

### #56 — Delta Review: Pre-seed AI with campaign roster at load time

**Original problem:** `sendCorrectionToAI()` had no awareness of existing campaign entities. AI could propose creating duplicates of existing NPCs, locations, etc.

**What was done:**
- #79: campaign entity names (NPCs, locations, monsters, combats) injected into the **OCR prompt** — prevents OCR misreads of known entity names
- #86: PARTY ROSTER (6 PCs with pc_ids) injected into **ROUND item correction context** — prevents AI from misattributing slots to wrong characters during structural corrections

**What was NOT done (original spec):**
- The full campaign roster (NPCs, locations, quests, factions, cohorts, bestiary) has NOT been injected into the AI correction sidebar system prompt for all item types
- `sendCorrectionToAI()` does not receive `window.campaignRoster` as described in #56
- NPC/location matching against existing entries is not grounded at correction time

**Recommendation:** Close #56 with a note summarising what was done and what wasn't, and file a new scoped issue covering the specific remaining gap: injecting the full campaign entity roster into `sendCorrectionToAI()` system prompt for all item types (not just ROUND items and not just OCR context). The original issue conflated two separate things that are better tracked separately.

---
